content.text:
{
  "module": "collaboration",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 42,
    "domain_depth": 35,
    "data_richness": 28,
    "interaction_quality": 45,
    "ai_integration": 15,
    "mobile_first": 32,
    "performance": 40,
    "typescript_quality": 52,
    "error_handling": 48,
    "realtime": 55,
    "security": 44,
    "differentiation": 38,
    "enterprise_readiness": 30
  },
  "overall_score": 39,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C7-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "No real team directory or project member management UI",
      "file": "src/pages/Directory.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires a searchable directory with company grouping, click-to-call, insurance status, linked RFIs/tasks, and CSV import. The collaboration module has presence and edit locks but no actual team member directory page with construction-specific fields (trade, company, certifications). This is the #1 missing feature for the module.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Directory.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The Directory page likely shows a basic list or placeholder without construction-specific fields like trade, company, insurance certificate status, linked RFIs, or company grouping. AFTER: Build a full Directory page with: (1) MetricBox row at top showing Total Contacts, Active Companies, Expiring Certifications, Missing Insurance counts. (2) A toggle between 'People' and 'Companies' views. (3) People view: a searchable table (use theme tokens from src/styles/theme.ts) with columns Name, Company, Role/Title, Trade, Phone (click-to-call link), Email (mailto link), Status (active/inactive Tag). Each row hoverable and clickable to open a detail slide-in panel. (4) Companies view: cards grouped by company showing company name, trade, contact count, insurance status (green dot for current, red for expired/missing). (5) Search bar at top with debounced 200ms filtering across name, company, email, phone, trade. (6) Empty state: illustration icon (Users from lucide-react), message 'Build your project directory. Add every stakeholder so your team always knows who to call.', CTA button 'Add First Contact'. (7) Use mock data with 12 realistic construction contacts across 5 companies (Turner GC, MEP Solutions, Steel Fabricators Inc, Pacific Concrete, Valley Electric) with construction trades and roles. (8) All styles use theme.colors, theme.spacing, theme.typography from src/styles/theme.ts. No hyphens in UI text. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C7-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "PresenceBar shows no construction-context activity indicators",
      "file": "src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx",
      "description": "The PresenceBar shows generic presence avatars but lacks construction-specific context. Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA, presence indicators should show what action users are performing (viewing RFI, editing daily log, reviewing submittal). The current implementation shows user dots but no activity labels like 'Reviewing RFI-023' or 'Editing Daily Log'.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx, make these specific changes: In the inner PresenceBarContent component where it renders user avatars, find the section that maps over visible users to render avatar circles. BEFORE: Each avatar shows only the user's initials and a tooltip with their name. The tooltip content is just the user name with no activity context. AFTER: (1) Enhance the tooltip content to show the user's current activity if available from presenceStore. Below the user name, add a line showing their current action in muted text (fontSize 11, color colors.textTertiary). Example: 'Mike Chen' on first line, 'Viewing RFI 023' on second line. (2) Read the user's currentEntity from the presence data (already available via usePresenceStore). If the user has a currentEntity set, format it as a readable label: map entity types to display names ('rfi' to 'RFI', 'submittal' to 'Submittal', 'daily_log' to 'Daily Log', 'punch_item' to 'Punch Item', 'change_order' to 'Change Order'). Show 'Viewing {EntityType} {entityId.slice(0,8)}' or just 'Viewing {EntityType}' if no ID. (3) Add a small colored dot (6px) next to each avatar indicating activity: green (#4EC896) for actively editing, blue (#3B82F6) for viewing, gray (#9CA3AF) for idle (lastSeen > 5 minutes ago). Position the dot at bottom-right of the avatar circle with position absolute. (4) Keep all existing error boundary and responsive behavior intact. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C7-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "EditConflictGuard modal lacks ARIA dialog pattern and focus trap",
      "file": "src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx",
      "description": "The conflict resolution dialog rendered by EditConflictGuard does not use role='alertdialog', aria-modal, aria-labelledby, or aria-describedby attributes. There is no focus trap, so keyboard users can tab behind the overlay. Screen readers cannot announce the conflict warning. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require all modals to be keyboard navigable with focus trapped.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx, make these specific changes: Find the conflict resolution overlay/modal JSX (the div that renders when conflictDetected is true, containing the warning message and action buttons). BEFORE: The overlay is a plain div with no ARIA attributes, no role, no focus management. Users can tab behind it. Screen readers do not announce it as a dialog. AFTER: (1) Add role='alertdialog' to the overlay container div. (2) Add aria-modal='true' to the overlay container. (3) Add an id like 'conflict-title' to the heading element and reference it with aria-labelledby='conflict-title' on the container. (4) Add an id like 'conflict-desc' to the description paragraph and reference it with aria-describedby='conflict-desc' on the container. (5) Add a useEffect that focuses the first actionable button when the conflict dialog appears. Use a ref on the primary action button (e.g. 'Refresh' or 'Keep My Changes') and call ref.current?.focus() when conflictDetected becomes true. (6) Add an onKeyDown handler on the overlay container that traps Tab focus: when Tab is pressed on the last focusable element, move focus to the first; when Shift+Tab on the first, move to the last. Identify focusable elements as buttons within the dialog. (7) Ensure the Escape key calls dismissConflict if that function exists. (8) Add aria-live='assertive' to the warning message text so screen readers announce it immediately. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C7-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "EntityPresence overflow dropdown not touch-friendly and lacks 44px targets",
      "file": "src/components/collaboration/EntityPresence.tsx",
      "description": "The PresenceDots component's overflow dropdown has small click targets (avatars are rendered without minimum 44x44px touch targets on the dropdown items). The overflow button and dropdown items need to meet Apple HIG 44px minimum for field use. The dropdown also does not close on outside tap on mobile.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/collaboration/EntityPresence.tsx, make these specific changes: In the PresenceDots component, find the overflow dropdown section (where overflowViewers are rendered when overflowOpen is true). BEFORE: The overflow dropdown items have small touch targets. The individual avatar buttons in the visible array have minWidth/minHeight 44 (good), but the overflow dropdown items inside the dropdown panel likely render at smaller sizes without adequate padding. The dropdown does not close on outside taps. AFTER: (1) For each item in the overflow dropdown, ensure the clickable/focusable container div has minHeight: 44, minWidth: '100%', padding: '8px 12px', display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', gap: 8. This makes each dropdown row at least 44px tall for touch. (2) Add a useEffect that listens for 'mousedown' and 'touchstart' events on document. If the event target is outside overflowContainerRef.current, call setOverflowOpen(false). Clean up the listener on unmount or when overflowOpen changes. (3) In the overflow toggle button (the '+N' button), ensure it has minWidth: 44, minHeight: 44 explicitly set for touch friendliness. (4) Add borderRadius: borderRadius.lg to the dropdown panel and boxShadow: shadows.lg for visual polish. (5) Ensure the dropdown panel has a max-height of 220px with overflowY: 'auto' so it does not overflow the screen on mobile with many viewers. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C7-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "enterprise_readiness",
      "title": "No meeting management page with agenda, minutes, and action item tracking",
      "file": "src/pages/Meetings.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 13 requires a full Meetings page with meeting series, agenda management, attendance tracking, action items with carry-forward, minutes generation, and calendar sync. The collaboration module currently has presence and edit locks but no Meetings page. This is critical for OAC meetings which happen weekly on every commercial project.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Meetings.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The Meetings page likely shows a placeholder or basic list without construction-specific meeting workflows. AFTER: Build a complete Meetings page with: (1) MetricBox row: Upcoming Meetings (count), Open Action Items (count), Meetings This Week (count), Avg Attendance Rate (percentage). Use Card and MetricBox from src/components/Primitives. (2) Two tabs: 'Upcoming' and 'Past' meetings. (3) Meeting list as cards showing: title, date/time, type badge (OAC in blue, Internal in gray, Safety in amber, Subcontractor in green), attendee avatar stack (max 4 + overflow count), action items count (open/total), location text. Each card hoverable with border color change on hover. (4) 'Schedule Meeting' primary button (Btn from Primitives, orange) in top right. (5) Mock data: 6 meetings including 'Weekly OAC Meeting #12' (type: OAC, 8 attendees, 3/5 action items), 'Daily Safety Standup' (type: Safety, 12 attendees, 1/1 action items), 'MEP Coordination Meeting' (type: Subcontractor, 6 attendees, 4/7 action items), and 3 past meetings with 'completed' status. (6) Empty state: Calendar icon, 'No meetings scheduled. Set up your recurring OAC meeting or schedule a one off.', CTA 'Schedule Meeting'. (7) Action items section below meeting list: a compact table showing description, assignee, due date, status (Tag component: open in amber, completed in green), source meeting name. Filter to show only open items by default. (8) All styles use theme tokens. No hyphens in UI text. All dollar amounts use proper formatting. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "AI Meeting Minutes Generator",
      "description": "After a meeting ends, one-click generates formatted PDF minutes from the agenda items, notes, and action items. AI summarizes discussion points into concise bullets and auto-extracts action items with assignees and due dates. Integrates with the ai-copilot edge function using the meeting context.",
      "impact": "Saves PMs 30+ minutes per OAC meeting. Procore has no equivalent. Fieldwire has no meetings module at all."
    },
    {
      "title": "Smart Notification Routing by Construction Role",
      "description": "Notifications learn which alerts each role cares about. Superintendents get punch list and daily log reminders. PMs get RFI overdue and change order alerts. CFOs get pay app and budget variance notifications. Subcontractors get only their assigned items. Reduces notification noise by 60% compared to 'notify everyone' approach.",
      "impact": "Directly addresses founder priority for smart notifications that suppress noise. No competitor has role-aware notification intelligence."
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's collaboration is centered on email-reply workflows (architects respond to RFIs without logging in) and unlimited users per project. Their presence and real-time features are minimal. SiteSync's Liveblocks-based presence and edit conflict guards are technically superior, but the module lacks the foundational pages (Directory, Meetings) that Procore ships out of the box. Fieldwire has excellent task-to-plan linking but zero meeting management. Autodesk BIM 360 has basic meetings but no action item carry-forward. The gap is clear: build the Directory and Meetings pages with construction-specific depth, and the real-time presence becomes a powerful differentiator layered on top.",
  "summary": "The Collaboration module has solid technical infrastructure for real-time presence (Liveblocks integration, PresenceBar, EntityPresence) and optimistic edit conflict resolution (EditConflictGuard with server timestamp verification). However, it is missing the two foundational pages that construction teams use daily: a project Directory with company grouping, trade fields, insurance tracking, and contact management, and a Meetings page with OAC meeting series, agendas, action item tracking, and minutes generation. The presence components need construction-context enrichment (showing what entity each user is viewing/editing) and accessibility improvements (ARIA dialog patterns on conflict modals, 44px touch targets on mobile). The module scores lowest on data richness (28), enterprise readiness (30), and mobile-first (32) because the real-time infrastructure exists but has no construction-domain content to operate on. Building the Directory and Meetings pages would immediately lift domain_depth, data_richness, and enterprise_readiness by 15-20 points each."
}
